# Maintenance Agreement Contract Templates

Ready-to-customize contract templates for local service businesses. Fill in bracketed fields with business-specific information.

---

## Template 1: Residential Service Agreement (General — Any Trade)

This template works for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, pest control, roofing, landscaping, and other home service trades. Customize the "Services Included" section for your specific trade.

---

### RESIDENTIAL MAINTENANCE SERVICE AGREEMENT

**Agreement Number:** [AUTO-GENERATED or MANUAL #]

**Effective Date:** _______________

---

#### PARTIES

**Service Provider ("Company"):**
- Company Name: [BUSINESS LEGAL NAME]
- DBA: [DOING BUSINESS AS, if different]
- Address: [STREET ADDRESS, CITY, STATE, ZIP]
- Phone: [PRIMARY PHONE]
- Email: [BUSINESS EMAIL]
- License #: [STATE/LOCAL LICENSE NUMBER]
- Insurance: [POLICY NUMBER / CARRIER]

**Customer ("Member"):**
- Name: _______________________________________________
- Service Address: _____________________________________
- City: _________________ State: _______ Zip: __________
- Mailing Address (if different): _______________________
- Phone (Primary): ________________ (Alternate): ________________
- Email: _______________________________________________

---

#### 1. PLAN SELECTION

The Customer selects the following maintenance plan:

| | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Monthly Rate | $________/mo | $________/mo | $________/mo |
| Annual Rate (____% discount) | $________/yr | $________/yr | $________/yr |
| **Selected** | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |

**Billing Preference:** [ ] Monthly Auto-Pay | [ ] Annual Prepaid

**Number of Systems/Units Covered:** ________

**Multi-System Discount Applied:** [ ] Yes — ____% | [ ] No

**Total Monthly/Annual Amount:** $________________

---

#### 2. TERM AND RENEWAL

**A. Initial Term.** This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues for a period of twelve (12) months ("Initial Term").

**B. Auto-Renewal.** Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month periods ("Renewal Terms") unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

**C. Renewal Notice.** Company shall send Customer a written renewal notice no later than sixty (60) days prior to the renewal date, stating the renewal terms and any changes to pricing or benefits.

**D. Price Changes.** Company reserves the right to adjust plan pricing upon renewal. Customer will be notified of any price changes in the renewal notice. If Customer does not cancel within thirty (30) days of receiving the renewal notice, the new pricing shall apply.

---

#### 3. SERVICES INCLUDED

The following services are included in the Customer's selected plan at no additional charge beyond the plan fee:

**[CUSTOMIZE THIS SECTION FOR YOUR TRADE AND TIERS]**

_Essential Plan Includes:_
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)

_Preferred Plan Includes (all Essential benefits plus):_
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)

_Priority Plan Includes (all Preferred benefits plus):_
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)
- [ ] ___________________________________________ (frequency: _________)

**Member Benefits:**
| Benefit | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---------|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Repair Discount | ____% | ____% | ____% |
| Priority Scheduling | No | Yes | Yes |
| No Diagnostic/Trip Fee | No | ____ | ____ |
| After-Hours/Emergency Fee Waived | No | No | ____ |
| Transferable to New Property Owner | No | No | ____ |

**Scheduling Windows:**
- [Service Type 1] scheduled during: [MONTH] through [MONTH]
- [Service Type 2] scheduled during: [MONTH] through [MONTH]
- Company will contact Customer at least [7/14] days in advance to schedule visits.
- Customer is responsible for providing access to equipment and service areas during scheduled appointments.

---

#### 4. SERVICES EXCLUDED

This Agreement does NOT cover and Customer is responsible for the full cost of:

a) Equipment replacement or new installation
b) Repairs necessitated by misuse, neglect, abuse, or unauthorized modification
c) Repairs necessitated by acts of God, natural disasters, power surges, or events beyond Company's control
d) Code compliance upgrades required by local, state, or federal authorities
e) Cosmetic damage or issues not affecting system function
f) Damage caused by third parties (other contractors, tenants, etc.)
g) [TRADE-SPECIFIC EXCLUSION: e.g., "Refrigerant recharging," "Sewer line replacement," "Structural roof damage"]
h) [TRADE-SPECIFIC EXCLUSION: e.g., "Ductwork modification," "Water main repair," "Termite damage remediation"]
i) Any service not expressly listed in Section 3 above

---

#### 5. REPAIR DISCOUNT TERMS

**A. Discount Application.** The repair discount listed in Section 3 applies to parts and labor for non-covered repairs performed by Company at the Service Address.

**B. Maximum Discount.** The repair discount applies to up to $________ in combined parts and labor charges per service visit. Charges exceeding this amount are billed at standard rates.

**C. Exclusions.** The repair discount does not apply to:
- Equipment replacement or new installations
- Services performed by subcontractors
- Manufacturer warranty work
- Other active promotions or coupons (discounts do not stack)

**D. Discount Eligibility.** The repair discount is available only while the Customer's plan is current and in good standing (no outstanding balance).

---

#### 6. PRIORITY SCHEDULING AND RESPONSE TIMES

**A. Priority Scheduling (Preferred and Priority Plans).** Company will schedule Preferred and Priority members ahead of non-member customers, subject to availability.

**B. Response Time Commitments:**
- Essential Plan: Within [3-5] business days of request
- Preferred Plan: Next business day (during normal business hours)
- Priority Plan: Same business day when request is received before [12:00 PM / 2:00 PM]; next business day for later requests

**C. Emergency Service (Priority Plan Only).** Company will respond to emergency service requests within [2-4] hours during normal business hours and within [4-8] hours outside of normal business hours. An "emergency" is defined as: [DEFINE: e.g., "complete system failure during extreme weather," "active water leak causing property damage," "gas leak or carbon monoxide detection"].

**D. Business Hours.** Company's normal business hours are [Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM] [and Saturday, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM]. Hours may vary on holidays.

---

#### 7. PAYMENT TERMS

**A. Monthly Plans.** Payment is due on the [1st / 15th / date of enrollment] of each month via automatic charge to the credit card, debit card, or bank account ("ACH") on file.

**B. Annual Plans.** Full payment is due at the time of enrollment or renewal.

**C. Payment Methods Accepted:** [ ] Visa [ ] Mastercard [ ] Amex [ ] Discover [ ] ACH/Bank Draft [ ] Check (annual only)

**D. Failed Payments.** If a scheduled payment fails, Company will:
1. Attempt to process the payment again within [3] business days
2. Notify Customer of the failed payment via [email / phone / text]
3. Retry payment up to [2] additional times over the following [10] days
4. If payment remains uncollected after [15] days, Customer's plan benefits are suspended
5. If payment remains uncollected after [30] days, this Agreement may be terminated per Section 8

**E. Past Due Balances.** Services will not be performed while any balance is past due. Plan benefits (discounts, priority scheduling) are suspended until the account is brought current.

**F. Late Fee.** A late fee of $________ [or ____% of the past-due amount] may be assessed on balances more than [15/30] days past due, to the extent permitted by applicable law.

---

#### 8. CANCELLATION AND REFUNDS

**A. Cancellation by Customer.** Customer may cancel this Agreement at any time by providing written notice (email, mail, or in-person) at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired cancellation date.

**B. Refund Policy for Annual Prepaid Plans.** If Customer cancels an annual prepaid plan before the term expires:
- [ ] **Option A — Pro-Rated Refund:** Customer will receive a refund of the unused portion of the annual fee, minus the retail value of any services already performed under the plan during the current term.
- [ ] **Option B — No Refund:** No refund will be issued for the unused portion of the annual fee. _(Check applicable state law — some states require pro-rated refunds.)_

**C. Refund Policy for Monthly Plans.** No refund is issued for the current month upon cancellation. Service continues through the end of the current billing cycle.

**D. Cancellation by Company.** Company may cancel this Agreement:
- Upon thirty (30) days written notice to Customer
- Immediately, if Customer's account is more than [30/60] days past due
- Immediately, if conditions at the Service Address pose a safety risk to Company's employees
- Immediately, if Customer engages in abusive, threatening, or fraudulent behavior

**E. Effect of Cancellation.** Upon cancellation, all plan benefits (discounts, priority scheduling, waived fees) cease immediately. Any outstanding balance remains due.

---

#### 9. SERVICE ADDRESS AND ACCESS

**A. Service Location.** All services under this Agreement will be performed at the Service Address listed above. Services at a different address require a separate agreement or written amendment.

**B. Access.** Customer agrees to provide Company with reasonable access to all equipment, systems, and areas necessary to perform the included services. This includes:
- Clear access paths to equipment (minimum [36] inches clearance)
- Restraint of pets during service visits
- Presence of an adult (18+) at the property during service, or provision of a key/code for access

**C. Access Denied.** If Company arrives for a scheduled service and is unable to access the property or equipment, a trip charge of $________ may apply, and the service will be rescheduled.

**D. Geographic Area.** This Agreement covers properties within Company's standard service area: [DEFINE: radius from shop, zip codes, city/county]. Properties outside this area may be subject to an additional travel charge of $________.

---

#### 10. LIMITATIONS AND WARRANTY

**A. Limitation of Liability.** Company's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by Customer during the current term. In no event shall Company be liable for incidental, consequential, or indirect damages.

**B. No Warranty of Equipment Condition.** This Agreement is a maintenance service agreement, not an equipment warranty or insurance policy. Company does not guarantee that equipment will not break down or require repair. Company warrants that included maintenance services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner.

**C. Pre-Existing Conditions.** Company is not responsible for pre-existing defects, code violations, or equipment conditions that existed prior to the Effective Date. If a pre-existing condition is discovered during a covered service, Company will notify Customer and provide an estimate for correction at the applicable member discount rate.

**D. Manufacturer Warranties.** This Agreement does not replace or extend any manufacturer warranty. Customer is responsible for understanding and maintaining any applicable manufacturer warranties.

---

#### 11. TRANSFERABILITY

**A. Transfer of Agreement.**
- [ ] **Non-Transferable.** This Agreement may not be transferred or assigned.
- [ ] **Transferable (Priority Plan Only).** This Agreement may be transferred to a new property owner upon written notice to Company and payment of a transfer fee of $________ [or "no transfer fee"]. The new owner must agree in writing to the terms of this Agreement.

**B. Customer Relocation.** If Customer moves to a new address within Company's service area, this Agreement may be transferred to the new address upon written notice. If Customer moves outside the service area, the cancellation terms in Section 8 apply.

---

#### 12. WHAT VOIDS THIS AGREEMENT

This Agreement shall be void and Company shall have no obligation to perform services if:

a) Customer fails to make payment as required and does not cure within [30] days of notice
b) Customer or any third party performs unauthorized modifications or repairs to covered equipment
c) Conditions at the Service Address are unsafe, unsanitary, or in violation of applicable codes to the extent that they prevent Company from safely performing services
d) Customer provides false or misleading information on this Agreement
e) Customer interferes with, threatens, or abuses Company's employees or subcontractors
f) Equipment is damaged beyond economical repair and Customer declines replacement

---

#### 13. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

**A. Informal Resolution.** The parties agree to attempt to resolve any dispute arising under this Agreement through good-faith negotiation.

**B. Mediation.** If negotiation is unsuccessful, the parties agree to submit the dispute to non-binding mediation before a mutually agreed-upon mediator, with costs shared equally.

**C. Governing Law.** This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of _________________.

**D. Venue.** Any legal action arising under this Agreement shall be brought in the courts of [COUNTY], [STATE].

---

#### 14. GENERAL PROVISIONS

**A. Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements, representations, and understandings.

**B. Amendments.** This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by both parties, or as provided in the renewal notice provisions of Section 2.

**C. Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

**D. Notices.** All notices under this Agreement shall be sent to the addresses listed above, via email, U.S. mail, or hand delivery.

**E. Independent Contractor.** Company is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, or partner of Customer.

---

#### SIGNATURES

By signing below, both parties agree to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

**Customer:**

Signature: _________________________________________ Date: _______________

Printed Name: _________________________________________

**Company Representative:**

Signature: _________________________________________ Date: _______________

Printed Name: _________________________________________

Title: _________________________________________

---

#### PAYMENT AUTHORIZATION

I authorize [BUSINESS NAME] to charge the following payment method for my maintenance plan fees:

**Payment Method:** [ ] Credit Card [ ] Debit Card [ ] ACH/Bank Draft

Card Number: ________________________________________ Exp: ______/______ CVV: ________

-OR-

Bank Name: _________________ Routing #: _________________ Account #: _________________

Cardholder/Account Holder Name: _________________________________________

Billing Address (if different from service address): __________________________________________

Signature: _________________________________________ Date: _______________

---

_This template is provided as a starting point. [BUSINESS NAME] should have this agreement reviewed by a licensed attorney in [STATE] before use. State consumer protection laws vary and may impose additional requirements for service agreements, auto-renewal disclosures, and refund obligations._

---
---

## Template 2: HVAC-Specific Maintenance Agreement

This template is tailored for HVAC companies with equipment-specific fields, seasonal tune-up details, and HVAC-relevant terms.

---

### HVAC MAINTENANCE SERVICE AGREEMENT

**Agreement Number:** [AUTO-GENERATED or MANUAL #]

**Effective Date:** _______________

---

#### PARTIES

**Service Provider ("Company"):**
- Company Name: [BUSINESS LEGAL NAME]
- DBA: [DOING BUSINESS AS, if different]
- Address: [STREET ADDRESS, CITY, STATE, ZIP]
- Phone: [PRIMARY PHONE]
- Email: [BUSINESS EMAIL]
- HVAC License #: [STATE HVAC CONTRACTOR LICENSE]
- EPA Certification: [608 CERTIFICATION TYPE]
- Insurance: [POLICY NUMBER / CARRIER]

**Customer ("Member"):**
- Name: _______________________________________________
- Service Address: _____________________________________
- City: _________________ State: _______ Zip: __________
- Mailing Address (if different): _______________________
- Phone (Primary): ________________ (Alternate): ________________
- Email: _______________________________________________

---

#### EQUIPMENT COVERED

List all HVAC systems covered under this Agreement:

**System 1 — Cooling:**
| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Equipment Type | [ ] Central AC [ ] Heat Pump [ ] Mini-Split [ ] Package Unit |
| Manufacturer | _________________________________ |
| Model Number | _________________________________ |
| Serial Number | _________________________________ |
| Approximate Age | ________ years |
| Tonnage/Capacity | ________ tons / ________ BTU |
| Refrigerant Type | [ ] R-410A [ ] R-22 [ ] R-32 [ ] Other: ________ |
| Location | [ ] Roof [ ] Ground [ ] Garage [ ] Attic [ ] Closet |
| Manufacturer Warranty Active | [ ] Yes — Exp: _________ [ ] No |

**System 1 — Heating:**
| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Equipment Type | [ ] Gas Furnace [ ] Electric Furnace [ ] Heat Pump [ ] Boiler [ ] Dual Fuel |
| Manufacturer | _________________________________ |
| Model Number | _________________________________ |
| Serial Number | _________________________________ |
| Approximate Age | ________ years |
| Capacity | ________ BTU input / ________ BTU output |
| Fuel Type | [ ] Natural Gas [ ] Propane [ ] Electric [ ] Oil |
| Location | [ ] Basement [ ] Attic [ ] Garage [ ] Closet [ ] Crawlspace |
| Manufacturer Warranty Active | [ ] Yes — Exp: _________ [ ] No |

**System 1 — Air Handler / Indoor Unit (if separate):**
| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Manufacturer | _________________________________ |
| Model Number | _________________________________ |
| Serial Number | _________________________________ |
| Filter Size | _________________________________ |
| Filter Type | [ ] Standard [ ] Pleated [ ] HEPA [ ] Electronic [ ] Media |

**Additional Systems:**

[ ] System 2 — attach additional equipment sheet
[ ] System 3 — attach additional equipment sheet
[ ] ________ total systems covered

---

#### PLAN SELECTION

| | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Monthly Rate (1 system) | $15/mo | $25/mo | $40/mo |
| Annual Rate (10% discount) | $162/yr | $270/yr | $432/yr |
| Additional System | +$10/mo | +$16/mo | +$25/mo |
| **Selected** | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |

**Number of Systems Covered:** ________

**Total Monthly Amount:** $________________

**Total Annual Amount:** $________________

**Billing Preference:** [ ] Monthly Auto-Pay [ ] Annual Prepaid

---

#### SERVICES INCLUDED BY PLAN

**SPRING / COOLING SEASON TUNE-UP** (Performed March through May)

All plans include the following air conditioning tune-up:

| Service | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---------|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Inspect and clean condenser coil | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Check refrigerant levels and pressures | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect electrical connections and tighten | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Test capacitors and contactors | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Lubricate moving parts | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect and clean condensate drain | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Check thermostat calibration and operation | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect ductwork for visible damage or leaks | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Measure supply and return air temperatures | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Check blower motor and amperage draw | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect evaporator coil (where accessible) | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Test safety controls and switches | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Measure airflow (static pressure) | -- | -- | Yes |
| Indoor air quality assessment | -- | -- | Yes |

**FALL / HEATING SEASON TUNE-UP** (Performed September through November)

| Service | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---------|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Full heating system tune-up | NOT INCLUDED | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect heat exchanger (gas/oil) | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Check gas pressure and burner operation | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Test ignition system | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect flue/venting for proper draft | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Test carbon monoxide levels at registers | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Check thermostat heating operation | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect blower motor and assembly | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Lubricate moving parts | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Check electrical connections | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Test safety controls (limit, rollout, pressure) | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Inspect condensate drain (high-efficiency units) | -- | Yes | Yes |
| Full carbon monoxide safety inspection | -- | -- | Yes |
| Combustion analysis | -- | -- | Yes |
| Ductwork inspection (accessible areas) | -- | -- | Yes |

**MEMBER BENEFITS**

| Benefit | Essential | Preferred | Priority |
|---------|:---------:|:---------:|:--------:|
| Repair discount (parts and labor) | 10% | 15% | 20% |
| No diagnostic / trip fee | -- | Yes ($______ value) | Yes ($______ value) |
| Priority scheduling | -- | Next business day | Same day |
| After-hours / weekend fee waived | -- | -- | Yes ($______ value) |
| Emergency response guarantee | -- | -- | Within _______ hours |
| Indoor air quality check | -- | -- | Annual |
| Filter delivery | -- | -- | Quarterly (sizes: __________) |
| Equipment replacement credit | -- | -- | $500 toward new system |
| Transferable to new homeowner | -- | -- | Yes |

---

#### HVAC-SPECIFIC EXCLUSIONS

In addition to the general exclusions, this Agreement does NOT cover:

a) **Refrigerant.** Refrigerant recharging or leak repair. If a refrigerant leak is detected during a tune-up, Company will notify Customer and provide an estimate for repair at the applicable member discount.

b) **Ductwork.** Duct repair, replacement, modification, sealing, or cleaning (unless specifically listed as an included service above).

c) **Equipment Replacement.** Replacement of any HVAC equipment including but not limited to: compressors, condenser units, air handlers, furnaces, heat pumps, coils, or thermostats. The Priority Plan equipment replacement credit is applied as a discount toward a new system installed by Company, not as a cash payment.

d) **R-22 Refrigerant.** Systems using R-22 (Freon) refrigerant: R-22 is no longer manufactured and availability is limited. If R-22 recharging is needed, Customer will be billed at current market rates. Company may recommend system replacement as a more cost-effective option.

e) **Zoning Systems.** Repair or replacement of zoning dampers, zone boards, or zone-specific thermostats beyond basic inspection.

f) **Water Damage.** Damage caused by condensate drain overflow, frozen pipes, or other water-related issues, including mold remediation.

g) **Permits.** Any work requiring permits from local authorities. Permit fees are Customer's responsibility.

h) **Pre-Existing Conditions.** Defects, damage, or improper installations that existed prior to the Effective Date or that are discovered during the initial tune-up. Company will document and report pre-existing conditions to Customer.

---

#### EQUIPMENT REPLACEMENT CREDIT (Priority Plan Only)

**A. Credit Amount.** Priority Plan members receive a $500 credit toward the purchase and installation of a new HVAC system from Company.

**B. Eligibility.** The credit is available after twelve (12) months of continuous Priority Plan membership.

**C. Application.** The credit is applied as a line-item discount on the installation invoice. It is not redeemable for cash, is not transferable, and may not be combined with other equipment promotions unless Company specifically authorizes it in writing.

**D. One-Time Use.** The credit may be used once per membership term. It resets upon renewal.

**E. System Requirements.** The replacement system must be purchased from and installed by Company. The credit does not apply to equipment purchased elsewhere or installed by a third party.

---

#### FILTER DELIVERY (Priority Plan Only)

**A. Filter Sizes Covered:** _______________________________________________

**B. Delivery Schedule:** Quarterly (every 3 months) beginning [DATE or "30 days after enrollment"]

**C. Filter Type:** Standard pleated filters (MERV 8-11). Upgrades to MERV 13+ or specialty filters available at additional cost.

**D. Delivery Method:** [ ] Shipped to Service Address [ ] Left at service visits [ ] Available for pickup at Company location

**E. Custom Sizes.** Non-standard filter sizes may require special ordering and a surcharge of $________ per filter.

---

#### SEASONAL SCHEDULING

**A. Spring Tune-Up Window.** Company will contact Customer between March 1 and May 31 to schedule the cooling season tune-up. Customer should respond within [7] days to secure their preferred date.

**B. Fall Tune-Up Window (Preferred and Priority Plans).** Company will contact Customer between September 1 and November 30 to schedule the heating season tune-up.

**C. Scheduling Priority.** Priority Plan members are scheduled first, followed by Preferred Plan members, followed by Essential Plan members. Within each tier, scheduling is first-come, first-served.

**D. Missed Appointments.** If Customer misses or cancels a scheduled tune-up without [24/48] hours notice, a trip charge of $________ may apply. If Customer fails to schedule their tune-up within the seasonal window, the tune-up is forfeited for that season and no credit or refund will be issued.

**E. Customer Obligation.** Customer is responsible for:
- Ensuring a clear path to all equipment (minimum 36 inches)
- Replacing or having available the correct filter size prior to the visit (unless filter delivery is included)
- Having an adult (18+) present or providing access instructions
- Restraining pets during the service visit

---

#### PAYMENT, CANCELLATION, AND GENERAL TERMS

The following terms apply to this Agreement and are incorporated by reference:

**Payment Terms:**
- Monthly payments are charged automatically on the [1st / 15th / enrollment date] of each month
- Accepted methods: [ ] Visa [ ] Mastercard [ ] Amex [ ] Discover [ ] ACH
- Failed payments: 3 retry attempts over 10 days, then benefits suspended
- Past-due accounts: benefits suspended until current, cancellation after 30 days past due
- Late fee: $________ per occurrence (where permitted by law)

**Auto-Renewal:**
- This Agreement auto-renews for successive 12-month terms
- Company sends renewal notice at least 60 days before renewal date
- Customer may opt out by providing 30 days written notice before renewal date
- Price adjustments communicated in renewal notice

**Cancellation:**
- Customer may cancel with 30 days written notice (email, mail, or in-person)
- Annual prepaid: [ ] Pro-rated refund minus retail value of services rendered [ ] No refund
- Monthly: no refund for current month; service continues through billing cycle
- All benefits cease upon cancellation effective date

**Limitation of Liability:**
- Company's total liability limited to fees paid in current term
- No liability for incidental, consequential, or indirect damages
- Company warrants services performed in a professional and workmanlike manner
- This is a maintenance agreement, not an equipment warranty or insurance policy

**Dispute Resolution:**
- Good-faith negotiation first, then non-binding mediation
- Governed by the laws of the State of _________________
- Venue: [COUNTY], [STATE]

**Transferability:**
- Essential and Preferred: Non-transferable
- Priority: Transferable to new property owner with written notice; transfer fee: $________ [or "waived"]
- Relocation within service area: Agreement transfers to new address with written notice

---

_This agreement template is provided as a starting point and should be reviewed by a licensed attorney in your state before use. HVAC licensing requirements, consumer protection laws, auto-renewal disclosure rules, and refund obligations vary by state. Ensure compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local regulations._

---

## Usage Notes for Both Templates

### Customization Checklist

Before using either template with actual customers:

1. **Fill in all bracketed fields** — company name, address, license numbers, pricing, etc.
2. **Choose your refund policy** (pro-rated or no refund) and check your state's laws
3. **Set your late fee amount** — check state usury/late fee limits
4. **Define your service area** — radius, zip codes, or city/county boundaries
5. **Define "emergency"** for Priority Plan response time commitments
6. **Set trip charge amounts** for missed appointments and access-denied situations
7. **Have an attorney review** the completed agreement for compliance with state law
8. **Create a one-page summary** version for field use (techs carry this, full agreement is emailed/mailed)

### State-Specific Considerations

These vary widely. Key areas to verify with local counsel:

- **Auto-renewal disclosure requirements** — Many states require specific language, font size, or separate signature for auto-renewal clauses (CA, NY, IL, VA, OR, and others)
- **Cancellation/refund rights** — Some states mandate pro-rated refunds or cooling-off periods for home service contracts
- **Home solicitation rules** — If signing up customers at their home, federal and state "cooling off" rules may apply (3-day right to cancel)
- **Payment card surcharge rules** — Some states prohibit credit card surcharges
- **Contractor licensing** — Ensure license numbers are current and displayed as required
- **EPA regulations** — For HVAC: refrigerant handling, recovery, and documentation requirements

### Digital Signature Options

For paperless enrollment:

- **DocuSign, HelloSign, PandaDoc** — full e-signature platforms with templates
- **ServiceTitan** — built-in agreement signing in the field
- **Housecall Pro** — digital invoicing with agreement attachment
- **Jobber** — quote/invoice with terms attachment
- **Square** — contracts feature for recurring billing
- **JotForm / Typeform** — web forms with e-signature for simple plans

### Quick-Reference: What to Bring to Every Service Call

Techs should carry (physically or on their tablet/phone):

1. One-page plan comparison sheet (tier table from SKILL.md Section 3)
2. Simplified enrollment form (name, address, plan selection, payment info, signature)
3. Business cards with plan QR code or URL
4. Referral cards for existing members
5. The sales scripts from SKILL.md Section 6 (memorized, not read aloud)
